message:
fix(fcp-zapier): enforce base_url endpoint policy at configure boundary (resolves br-898o4)

Resolves flywheel_connectors-898o4 — [REVIEW-A9][P1] connector
security: configure() previously accepted base_url outside the
declared endpoint policy, so the api_key bearer token could be
routed to an attacker-controlled host.

**Previous behavior (token-exfil vector):**

`ZapierConfig::from_params` accepted ANY string as `base_url` and
constructed a `ZapierClient` (which carries the bearer token) BEFORE
any policy check fired. The `provisioning_readiness` view reported
the rejection through `network_ok = false` / `network_message`, but
that surface is informational — it did NOT fail configuration.
`handle_configure` would still succeed, and any subsequent invoke
would send Authorization: Bearer <api_key> to an attacker host.

**Fix:**

Promote the existing `base_url_policy` check to a hard refuse inside
`ZapierConfig::from_params`. If the base_url is not (https + zapier.com
host) and not a localhost/127.0.0.1 test allowance, return
`FcpError::InvalidRequest { code: 1003, message: <policy text> }`.
The `ZapierClient` (and the bearer token plumb-through) is never
constructed for an off-policy URL. Localhost/127.0.0.1 stay allowed
for tests via the unchanged `is_local_test_host` helper.

**Regression tests (4 new + 1 promoted, 1 inverted):**

- `from_params_rejects_non_zapier_https_host`: rejects
  `https://evil.example.com`, asserts the rejection names
  "zapier.com".
- `from_params_rejects_http_non_local_host`: rejects
  `http://api.zapier.com` (downgrade attempt — same host is
  rejected without https unless localhost).
- `from_params_accepts_localhost_for_tests`: locks in the
  localhost test-allowance so the fix doesn't break test
  fixtures.
- `from_params_accepts_default_zapier_url`: regression guard —
  if `DEFAULT_BASE_URL` ever drifts off-policy, this fails fast.

Existing tests adjusted:
- `config_custom_base_url` → `config_custom_base_url_within_policy`:
  was asserting `https://zapier.example.com/v1` (a NON-zapier.com
  host) was accepted; that was the bug. Now uses
  `https://api.zapier.com/v1` to lock in the policy-conforming path.
- `provisioning_readiness_network_rejected_custom_url` →
  `from_params_rejects_non_zapier_https_host`: since the rejection
  now happens at from_params construction, the assertion target
  moves from the readiness view to the construction Result.
- `tests/integration.rs::lifecycle_self_check_bad_network` →
  `lifecycle_configure_rejects_off_policy_base_url`: previously
  configured a malicious URL, asserted self_check reported
  failure AFTER configure succeeded. The inversion locks in the
  configure-time refuse.

200 fcp-zapier lib + 47 integration tests passing; 0 failures.

**Defense-in-depth note:**

The policy enforcement is symmetric to the registry-trust-roots
n4429 fix landed in f17bf191: in both, the previous code accepted
attacker-controlled values at the construction boundary and
expected a separate informational view to flag the issue. The
correct discipline is to fail closed at construction, not surface
the violation as a status code that callers may ignore.
